title: Testing, Static Quality, Performance, and Release · Runtime Trace: Test Behavior
summary: Harden the service-data client with behavioral tests, deterministic fixtures, performance evidence, and a clean release. Planned artifact: Add and verify the js test behavior increment in a tested and release-ready JavaScript application. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Design tests around observable behavior, changed inputs, boundaries, and failures rather than implementation lines.
Explain origin, CORS, CSP, credentials, workers, and permission prompts as layered browser boundaries.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

// javascript-basics cumulative stakeholder program.

steps.title: Predict Test Behavior
steps.instruction:
theory case — A release candidate passes the familiar 2-record fixture but fails an empty list and a duplicate identifier. Reproduce both, add behavior and accessibility regressions, and reject release until the clean build agrees.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Test Behavior contract for the supplied data.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for design tests around observable behavior, changed inputs, boundaries, and failures rather than implementation lines.

content.text: A release candidate passes the familiar 2-record fixture but fails an empty list and a duplicate identifier. Reproduce both, add behavior and accessibility regressions, and reject release until the clean build agrees.

content.text: Design tests around observable behavior, changed inputs, boundaries, and failures rather than implementation lines. Use this as an observable contract: name its preconditions, trace the state or value change, and state what evidence would disprove the result.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Trace concrete runtime values, bindings, receiver, mutation, and queued work in order; assert the observable result, then change one input or event and seek the first causal divergence.

content.title: Worked case pattern

A defensible solution applies Test Behavior, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to a tested and release-ready JavaScript application.

steps.title: Defend the Test Behavior model
steps.instruction: Write the theory evidence note for Test Behavior: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A release candidate passes the familiar 3-record fixture but fails an empty list and a duplicate identifier. Reproduce both, add behavior and accessibility regressions, and reject release until the clean build agrees.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Design tests around observable behavior, changed inputs, boundaries, and failures rather than implementation lines.
